source.definition: a large vehicle designed to carry many passengers

explanation.sourceText: Shortened from the English word 'omnibus', meaning 'for all', introduced in the 19th century.
explanation.targetText: Prescurtare din englezescul „omnibus“, care înseamnă „pentru toți“, introdus în secolul al XIX-lea.

source.additional: Buses are a common means of transportation in cities and rural areas.

target.definition: un vehicul mare care transportă mulți pasageri

explanation.sourceText: Derived from the French word 'autobus', which comes from the Latin 'omnibus', meaning 'for all'.
explanation.targetText: Provine din francezul „autobus“, derivat din latinescul „omnibus“, care înseamnă „pentru toți“.

target.additional: „Autobuz“ este un cuvânt frecvent folosit în transportul public.

explanation.sourceText: A large vehicle used for transporting people.
explanation.targetText: Un vehicul mare pentru transportul persoanelor.

examples.sourceText: The bus will arrive in five minutes.
The bus goes to the next stop.
We take the bus to the concert.
Please talk to the bus driver.
examples.targetText: Te rog să îi vorbești șoferului de autobuz.

examples.sourceText: I forgot my ticket on the bus.

learningTips.text: Remember 'autobuz' by thinking of 'auto' (car) plus 'buzz'—imagine a big car buzzing through the city carrying many people.
Think of 'autobuz' as the 'auto for a buzz of people'—a vehicle full of passengers creating a buzz.
Picture yourself riding an 'autobuz' with friends on a fun trip—this makes the word lively and easy to recall.
Connect the English word 'bus' with 'autobuz' by imagining a crowded city bus full of passengers.
